accepted.reason: Anagram signature/count grouping provides compact string-processing loops with optimization value.
Heap push/pop operations are reusable core data-structure kernels used in many optimization pipelines.
Lennard-Jones pair-energy accumulation is a canonical molecular simulation compute kernel.
SLIM-style SGD/MSE updates are practical recommender optimization kernels with tight loops.
Prime trial-division loops are simple but high-signal integer optimization workloads.
Flexure-style stencil updates are strong PDE-like kernels for Cython acceleration practice.
Sliding-window median filtering is a common signal/image processing kernel.
Piecewise linear interpolation is a broadly reusable numeric kernel with predictable control flow.

skipped.reason: MPI base internals are framework/integration glue rather than standalone optimization exemplars.
Distributed MPI-oriented task glue is too environment-coupled for generic conversion training.
